title: berkheya angolensis
intro: Berkheya angolensis, also known as the Devil's Backbone or Wild Bokkeveld, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Berkheya angolensis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions such as wounds and eczema. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and are traditionally used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southern Africa.
